techniques.name: LSASS Memory
techniques.evidence: Cobalt Strike injects into the LSASS process to steal cached credentials.

techniques.name: Exfiltration Over C2 Channel
techniques.evidence: The malware executes the initial discovery commands and then exfiltrates the results via the C2 channel.

techniques.name: PowerShell
techniques.evidence: The attackers use a PowerShell loader method to deploy a Beacon.

techniques.name: Windows Command Shell
techniques.evidence: The attackers execute batch scripts to disable antivirus programs.

techniques.name: Domain Accounts
techniques.evidence: The threat actors instructed IcedID to download and execute the next stage malware two hours after the initial compromise.

techniques.name: Exploitation for Client Execution
techniques.evidence: The attackers deployed XingLocker ransomware via wmic and batch scripts.

techniques.name: Regsvr32
techniques.evidence: A batch script schedules a task to execute a command to load a Cobalt Strike Beacon into memory using regsvr32.

techniques.name: Data Encrypted for Impact
techniques.evidence: The threat actors deployed XingLocker ransomware.

techniques.name: Launch Agent
techniques.evidence: The attackers used Cobalt Strike’s jump psexec_psh capability to move laterally.

techniques.name: Lateral Tool Transfer
techniques.evidence: The attackers downloaded and executed AdFind to collect further information about the domain.

techniques.name: Path Interception by Search Order Hijacking
techniques.evidence: The attackers used batch scripts for persistence, defense evasion and execution tasks.

techniques.name: Domains
techniques.evidence: The attackers had a script for persistence, defense evasion and execution tasks.

techniques.name: Disable or Modify Tools
techniques.evidence: The attackers executed batch scripts to disable antivirus programs.
